ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Cullen for and with Lottie Mayfield (Executor).

The Court noted that it received additional information regarding the issues that were raised by the Court and that there are no objections. The Court granted the First and Final Report on Waiver of Account; Petition for Final Distribution (order signed in open Court).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Continued To Jan-31-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-20-2016 Continued To Jan-17-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Cullen for and with (executor) Lottie Mayfield.

The Court stated that this is a petition for final distribution, that the issue goes to the request to approve a 50% contingency fee not approved by the court and that it is voided by 10813. Mr. Cullen reported that there was a civil matter, that he got involved at the last minute, that there was a long complicated litigation with a default against Ms. Mayfield, that he was able to have the default set aside, that they were successful at trial, that this was a contract that was agreed on, that Ms. Mayfield has expressed personal reasons to reduce the fee to 45% which counsel has agreed to do. The Court stated that its question is why approval was not sought for the contingency. Mr. Culled cited 11604, that it is the same as any beneficiary who makes an agreement to transfer interest and that a lot of work was done in the civil case. The Court stated that it would be useful for the court to be told of the time involvement in a further declaration. Mr. Cullen stated that he did not keep track of hours but that he can attempt to put down a reasonable assessment of hours. The Court stated that counsel would be well advised to keep track of hours, that whatever information he could provide would be helpful to support the reasonableness of the fees. Mr. Cullen stated that at the time it did not appear that the estate had more that $50,000.00 in it. The petition for final distribution was continued to January 17, 2017 for further documentation and support.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Mr. Cullen reported that the filing of the petition for final distribution is still being held up pending a ruling in the civil proceeding before Judge Carson. PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-22-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Cullen for and with Lottie Mayfield. LeRoy Rice for the Estate of William Sykes.

The Court stated that the matter was on for the Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, that there was an objection by filed by Ms. Nora (the Administrator of William Sykes Estate), that there were trial briefs due, and that the Court only received one from Mr. Cullen. Ms. Rice addressed his non-appearance at the last hearing, stated that he only received three days notice to prepare a trial brief, that he didn't have the facilities to prepare one with such short notice, that this matter has been set for trial many times because of the history with Ms. Mayfield, and that with all the continuances and "ins and outs" that he never thought it would get to trial. The Court informed Mr. Rice that he was present on 7/9/10, that he said that he was available on the very date the matter was set for trial, and after additional discussions with Mr. Rice stated that it was going forward today with the trial.

9:15 am There being no opening statements by Counsel, Mr. Cullen (for Petitioner) calls Lottie Mayfield, who is sworn and examined under direct examination.
9:23 am Cross-examination by Mr. Rice for Respondent.
9:25 am Re-direct examination.
9:27 am There being no further questions, Ms. Mayfield is excused and the Petitioner rests.
9:28 am There being no evidence by Mr. Rice, Mr. Rice submits with objections.
9:30 am The matter is submitted over Mr. Rice's objections. The Court finds under Probate Code 8223 that the Petitioner has met her burden notwithstanding the objections and grants the petition for the reasons set forth on the record. Mr. Cullen is to prepare the order and submit it to Mr. Rice for approval as to form.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-10-2010 Continued To Sep-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Cullen for and with Lottie Mayfield.

Mr. Cullen informed the Court that he was substituting in for Lottie Mayfield, that Mr. Rice had a conflict and would not be appearing today, that he had prepared a stipulation and sent it to Mr. Rice to sign, but apparently Mr. Rice was not in his office. Discussions regarding Mr. Rice's non-appearance were held. Mr. Cullen stated that he had the substitution on him and that it would be filed today. The Court addressed the fact that trial briefs were due on 9/3/10 and to date none had been filed. Mr. Cullen stated that he only entered back in to the matter this Wednesday and that he was unaware of the deadline. The Court continued the matter to September 24, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and instructed Mr. Cullen to serve Mr. Rice with the date and time of the hearing. The Court also ordered trial briefs by Counsel no later than the end of business on September 17, 2010.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda.

Ms. Mayfield requested a continuance for the purpose of obtaining Counsel, stated that she was out of town for 6 months caring for her Aunt, that she had not spoken to other Counsel but that she spoke to Mr. Cullen, and that there was a possibility of Mr. Cullen substituting back in. Mr. Rice did not object to a continuance. The Parties disagreed as to whether the prior settlement on Williams estate would reslove the will contest on Maggie's estate and as to the reasons why the settlement on Williams estate did not finalize. The matter was continued to September 10, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Trial briefs from both Parties are due by September 3, 2010. Ms. Mayfield will advise Mr. Cullen in the event that agrees to represent her and also provided her current P.O. Box address.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-15-2008 Continued To Feb-02-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Terry Mollica for HSBC Bank (Interested Party). John Cullen for Lottie Mayfield (Petitioner). Leroy Rice for the Estate of William Sykes.

Mr. Cullen stated that there has been an objection, that the original was not found, the will was given to Mr. Legalette, that the law firm was unable to locate the original will and they do not have an explanation, that he has spoken to Mr. Reed and he has no objection to issuing a subpoena to the law firm. The Court stated that according to the file the will was turned over to Mr. Legalette's attorney, and asked Mr. Cullen if he checked. Mr. Cullen stated that they did have possession at one time, they don't have the original will, and he received a letter which is what prompted all this. Mr. Rice stated they have no idea where the will is. Mr. Cullen stated that the law does provide for oral confirmation of the will. The Court stated that it needed a declaraion from Mr. Reed so it could move comfortably forward. The matter was continued to February 2, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. Mr. Mollica stated that this matter was before Judge Mellon and that the Court may want to examine his statement of decision because it would be re-opening an issue that was already adjudicated.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
